title: Level 132
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 132: "Words Left Unspoken"}} {{collapse|initial=|final=|body={{Audio|file=Level0.mp3|volume=0.2}}}}




{{Collapse|num=2|body=|initial=
Click page to change versions
[[File:Level132-1.jpg|center|thumb|450px|
You sure this isn't hopeless?
]]
Level 132 is a series of beige painted rooms, extremely similar to the basic visual appearance of Level 0. Structurally, it is wholly composed of boxes, rarely splitting off from one another. However, due to the seemingly infinite length of Level 132, there is an unknown number of split pathways. The various furniture found within Level 132 also reflects its generic office structuring, and may hold supplies such as Almond Water. Windows may be haphazardly placed in some locations, and are likely to be homes for Predatory Windows. Other hostile architecture has also been seen within Level 132, but are extremely rare in comparison to the windows. Sightings of Deathmoths and Smilers have been cited within the darker regions of Level 132. These darker rooms are often akin to supply closets, personal offices, and meetings rooms, with furniture to match. All of which, maintain the same beige tinted wallpaper, in perfect condition. [[File:Level132-2.png|center|thumb|450px|
I'm at a loss of words.
]] Over time, as one travels further into Level 132, the environment increasingly grows visually incoherent. These changes first start after a wanderer has gone through 100 rooms. Initially, the changes only effect the various features within, suspending them in the air or clipping into the ground. The walls and textures begin to distort 50 rooms later, changing color, design, and structure. Rooms become perfect spheres, and others are smothered with messy colors, as if a child had painted on the walls. Within another 200 rooms, descriptions of the level begin to falter. The few that have stepped into room 350 have stated it to be incommunicable. [[File:Level132-3.png|center|thumb|450px|
What else is there to say?
]] Some have explained how the amenities appear to invert on themselves, leaving a void of what it could be. Other wanderers have shared seeing the entities move with the precision of robots, fixed to the same lines. Here begins the unravelment of the psyche, as comprehension slowly ceases to work. Despite never feeling so bizarre and unique, a sense of eternity develops in those who travel too deep. They are afraid to leave, yet desperately wish for normality and uniformity. The curse of the liminal is potent and traumatic, as memories are slowly scrubbed away and replaced with nothing. No one lives on the other side, waiting to prove it's all a ruse. It is a greater sense of abandonment only compounded by the overwhelming loneliness, a combination of many phenomena. How are they to comprehend it all? It feels like eternity in there, it's an experience longer than you can think. Maybe they could find safety, perhaps another few rooms they could witness the smiles of those they knew. Even when the office finally reveals a bright sky, like a chick coming out of an egg. It's still too much, many who travel deep stay there. Do they find death at the end of the pathway, a way back into reality? Is it even worth finding out? [[File:Level132-4.jpg|center|thumb|450px|
No more.
]]
